Petition To Compel Trustee To Distribute Specific Gifts And To Account; For Breach Of Trust; For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee; For An Award Of
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Continued To Jan-08-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Szezepanik for and with Louis Neff, Jr. (Petitioner).
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Robert Mills (Respondent).

Mr. Szezepanik stated that he filed a status report on Tuesday, that it is undisputed that Mr. Neff is a Beneficiary of the Trust and entitled to the property on States Street in San Francisco, that Mr. Mills has refused to distribute specific assets designated to Mr. Neff and that the accounting which was ordered by the Court at the last hearing has not been provided. Mr. Szezepanik also stated that he served discovery, that the deadline to respond to discovery has passed, that he sent a letter to Mr. Vaught on July 7th regarding mediation and that he received no response, that Mr. Mills is a residuary beneficiary of the property, that the property is income generating and that irreparable harm is being done to his Client everyday. In addition, Mr. Szezepanik asked the Court to suspend Mr. Mills, that distribution be granted, and stated that his Client is incurring legal fees and is being deprived of income. Mr. Vaught stated that the Attorney for the Trust is working on the accounting, that he did not get the letter, that the discovery response is sitting on his desk and that he is finalizing it. Mr. Vaught also stated that he is prepared to go to mediation, that there are $1,500,000.00 in liens against the property, that they only have receipts for half of it, that he has repeatedly asked for cancelled checks from Mr. Neff and that he has not received anything. Mr. Szezepanik thinks this could be a one day trial. Mr. Vaught stated that it needs to be a three day trial. The Court stated that it will not grant Mr. Szezepanik's request without addressing the merits. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Distribute Specific Gifts, etc. is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on January 8, 2018 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court/a copy of the order was provided to Counsel). Status on mediation is set for October 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Vaught is ordered to make a personal appearance on that date. The Court orders that the accounting be provided by Friday September 1, 2017 or sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will imposed against the Trustee.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Distribute Specific Gifts And To Account; For Breach Of Trust; For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee; For An Award Of
Off Calendar Jun-26-2017 Continued To Aug-28-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Szezepanik for and with Louis Neff.
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Robert Mills (Respondent).

The Court denied Mr. Szezepanik's request to suspend the Trustee. The Parties indicated that they are willing to do private mediation with minimal discovery and discussed setting a hearing on the issue of undue influence within two to three months out. Mr. Vaught stated that he will provide an accounting. Said accounting is to be provided by August 14, 2017. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Distribute Specific Gifts and to Account, etc. is continued to August 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Distribute Specific Gifts And To Account; For Breach Of Trust; For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee; For An Award Of
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Jun-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Distribute Specific Gifts And To Account; For Breach Of Trust; For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee; For An Award Of
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Continued To Jun-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Szezepanik for and with Louis Neff Jr. (Petitioner).
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for Robert Mills (Respondent).

Mr. Vaught stated that he waives the requirement of service, that he has been trying to
e-file his response since Thursday and that he has a copy of the petition. The Court denied Mr. Szezepanik's request to grant the petition stating that it will not do so without the response being filed. The Petition Compel Trustee to Distribute Specific Gifts and to Account, etc. is continued to June 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
